title: Novo Nordisk Reports Cybersecurity Breach Affecting Clinical Trial Patient Data
summary: New Delhi: Novo Nordisk said on Thursday it has identified a ‚Äãsecurity incident in which ‚Äãcertain information, including patient data from some ‚Äãclinical trials, was copied externally without...
thbAction: Position THB's pharma CRM and omnichannel HCP engagement workflows to support launch planning, KAM orchestration, and compliant follow-up across India and the Middle East.
contentHtml:
New Delhi: Novo Nordisk said on Thursday it has identified a ‚Äãsecurity incident in which ‚Äãcertain information, including patient data from some ‚Äãclinical trials, was copied externally without authorization from its internal IT systems.

The company, maker of the blockbuster weight-loss drug Wegovy, said it has launched ‚Äåa probe ‚ÅÝwith the ‚ÅÝassistance of external cybersecurity experts and is in contact with the ‚Äãrelevant authorities.

"The incident affected a limited amount of information related to patients ‚Äãparticipating in some of our clinical trials," Novo said in its statement, without disclosing what type of trials.

The potential categories ‚Äãof personal data affected may include patient ‚ÅÝID, year ‚Äåof birth, sex and health or immunogenicity ‚Äãdata among ‚Äãothers, it added. Novo did not provide further details ‚ÅÝwhen contacted by Reuters.

In its statement, Novo said ‚Äãthe information is not directly linked to ‚Äãany patients by name or other direct identifiers, and it does not consider the incident to enable any third party to identify participants in its clinical trials.

Knowledge of patient identity would require access to further information, which was not ‚Äåpart of the incident, the company added.

The drugmaker said it does not view the incident as ‚Äãposing any immediate ‚Äãrisks to patients, ‚ÅÝbut advised that they report any unusual occurrences they believe could be linked to the incident.

The company has temporarily taken certain ‚Äãinternal IT systems offline, and is working to bring the affected systems back online in a controlled and safe manner.

The company's core business operations are not impacted and remain up and running, it added.

Source : Reuters

Sheeba Farhat Joined Medical Dialogues in 2018 to report on the latest Education news. A Graduate of the University of Delhi, she specializes in covering stories related to Medical Education updates. For inquiries or further information, you can reach her at editorial@medicaldialogues.in.
